assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and an email or social identity usable for Auth0 signup.
No Auth0 account, tenant, application, or framework quickstart has been selected yet.

developer_goal: Create an Auth0 account and tenant, then reach the first authenticated application or protected-API success for a chosen architecture and framework.

first_success.outcome: The selected quickstart's first authentication or authorization behavior succeeds.
first_success.observable_signal: Depends on the unresolved architecture; for example, a SPA sample showing Successfully authenticated and the user profile after login.

route_selection.surface: Auth0 Dashboard self-service account, tenant, and Quickstarts
route_selection.rule: Select the shortest complete route only after one application architecture and framework are fixed.

candidates.name: Single-Page App quickstart
candidates.reason: Auth0 lists SPA as a peer architecture class with many framework-specific guides.

candidates.name: Regular Web App quickstart
candidates.reason: Server-rendered apps use different credential handling and callback mechanics than SPA or native routes.

candidates.name: Native or Mobile App quickstart
candidates.reason: Native clients are a separate architecture with different SDKs and success checks.

candidates.name: Backend or API quickstart
candidates.reason: Protecting an API is a different first-success outcome from interactive login.

required_path.action: Create an Auth0 account using username or email or phone and password, or a social provider.

required_fields.label: Signup method and identity credentials
required_fields.notes: Docs list password and social options but do not publish the exact current required form fields.

required_path.observable_result: Auth0 creates an account and continues into tenant creation.

required_path.action: Create the first Auth0 tenant and choose a region locality.
required_path.observable_result: Auth0 provisions a tenant domain such as {name}.{locality}.auth0.com.

required_path.action: Create an Auth0 application of the selected type in the Dashboard.

required_fields.label: Application type
required_fields.notes: Native, SPA, Regular Web, or Machine-to-Machine.

required_path.observable_result: Auth0 assigns a client ID for the new application.

required_path.action: Complete the selected framework quickstart through first authentication or API authorization success.
required_path.observable_result: The selected sample reaches its documented authenticated terminal state.

prerequisites.description: An Auth0 account and tenant are required before applications can be registered.
The developer must choose an application architecture before a single Quickstart path exists.

external_gates.description: Social identity-provider authorization may be required when that signup method is chosen.

platform_outcomes.description: Auth0 creates the tenant domain and hosts authentication for the selected locality.
Auth0 assigns a client ID when an application is created.

excluded.item: Custom domains and multi-environment tenant strategy
excluded.reason: Custom domains are recommended for production and are not required for the first tenant getting-started path.

excluded.item: Reading pricing pages as a measured step
excluded.reason: Commercial plan comparison is outside the developer required path.

sources.title: Add Login to Your JavaScript Application
evidence_supported: One example SPA login success signal among many framework routes

sources.title: Auth0 Pricing
evidence_supported: Commercial plan context excluded from the measured path

uncertainties.question: Which Auth0 application architecture and framework define the workshop first-success route?
uncertainties.impact: Architecture choice changes credentials, callbacks, SDK work, and the observable success signal.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Choose one bounded Quickstart such as Vanilla JS SPA login, then verify signup, tenant, application, and sample steps on a fresh account.

uncertainties.question: What exact verification, consent, and anti-abuse fields appear on the current Auth0 signup UI?
uncertainties.impact: Hidden signup fields would change required field counts and may add email or captcha waits.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated public-UI capture or fresh-account walkthrough of the current Auth0 signup screens.
